chief_complaint: A 30-year-old woman presents with a 2-month history of thoracic pain.
history: The patient initially suspected costochondritis, but the pain was unresponsive to conservative management. Imaging revealed a paravertebral mass at the T6-T7 level. The differential diagnosis included malignancy, sarcoma, and tuberculosis (TB). A positive QuantiFERON-TB Gold test suggested TB exposure, before a biopsy confirmed spinal TB.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG: not mentioned, Bloods: QuantiFERON-TB Gold test positive, Imaging: paravertebral mass at the T6-T7 level, confirmed spinal TB by biopsy.
